name: Gardens by the Bay
description: Singapore's landscaping project is home to 18 incredible Supertrees.

name: Haw Par Villa
description: A Buddhist theme park brings you a Buddhist version of Hell.

name: Singapore's Rooftop Pool
description: The largest pool at its elevation sits on top of one of the most expensive resorts in the world.

name: Hawker Chan Soya Sauce Chicken Rice & Noodle
description: This Singaporean food-court stall was home to the world's cheapest Michelin-starred meal.

name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple & Museum
description: This $62 million dollar temple was erected to house one little dental fragment of religious history.

name: Changi Airport Slides
description: The busy transport hub is home to the tallest airport slide in the world.

name: Henderson Waves
description: The highest pedestrian bridge in Singapore looks like a series of undulating waves rising between two public parks.

name: House of Tan Teng Niah
description: This house is one of the most colorful in the country, and is the last remaining example of a historic villa.

name: World's First Salmon ATM
description: Frozen in Norway, vended in Singapore.

name: The Live Turtle and Tortoise Museum
description: Terrapins of all colors and shapes are quick to bring fascination (and possibly long life) at this museum.

name: Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve
description: This rare wildlife haven in Singapore feels like you're in the tropical rainforest.

name: Katong Antique House
description: This jam-packed Singapore antique museum preserves the fading remnants of a uniquely mixed culture.

name: Merlion
description: This statue once earned the distinction as one of the "Three Major Disappointments of the World."

name: Kampong Lorong Buangkok
description: Singapore's last surviving traditional village is hidden among giant skyscrapers and modern infrastructure.

name: St. Joseph's Church
description: The first Catholic church in Singapore is a Neo-Gothic colossus with a rich architectural history.

name: CHIJMES
description: A historical convent known for taking in bad luck babies left on its doorstep is now an upscale shopping center.

name: Krishna's Kitchen
description: Little India's hidden gem of kindness and good food.

name: Bukit Brown Cemetery
description: One of the largest Chinese cemeteries outside of China spent years overgrown and forgotten.

name: Changi Airport's Butterfly Garden
description: Watch butterflies take flight before you fly.

name: Bronze Bathers
description: The most playful of the life-sized statues that make up Singapore's "People of the River."

name: German Girl Shrine
description: A Singapore shrine to an unnamed German girl who has become a local deity.

name: Southernmost Point of Continental Asia
description: Across this single-file rope bridge is what has been claimed to be the edge of continental Asia.

name: Mint Museum of Toys
description: 30 years worth of pressed-tin, bakalite, and characters both loved and forgotten.

name: Kong Meng San Phor Kark See Monastery
description: The largest monastery in Singapore is home to a crematorium, a college, and one of Asia’s largest indoor Buddha statues.

name: Sri Mariamman Temple
description: Singapore's oldest Hindu temple.

name: Japanese Cemetery Park
description: From prostitutes to soldiers to business men, the dead in this graveyard are a microcosm of Singapore's history with Japan.

name: Little Guilin
description: A disused quarry turned lake in the middle of a residential district.

name: Berlin Wall in Singapore
description: Two panes of the massive Cold War artifact are on display at the National University of Singapore.

name: 'Planet'
description: A giant baby floats in the middle of Singapore’s nature park.

name: Singapore's Blinking Snails
description: Relax on your veranda and watch the light show.

name: Kwong Wai Siew Peck San Theng
description: The confluence of modernist and traditional architecture in this religious complex reflects the history of both the living and the dead being uprooted, displaced, and rehoused.

name: Fountain of Wealth
description: This fountain was once heralded as the largest in the world.

name: Kebun Baru Birdsinging Club
description: Singapore's Ang Mo Kio Town Garden West is a social hotspot for songbirds and their humans.

name: Shuang Long Shan Cemetery
description: The last remaining Hakka cemetery in Singapore.

name: Soy Sauce Bottles of Sultan Mosque
description: A ring of soy sauce bottle bases decorate an important Singaporean landmark.

name: Thow Kwang Pottery Jungle
description: This family-owned pottery shop is home to the last operational dragon kiln in Singapore.

name: Prisoner of War Steps
description: The history of those incarcerated in Singapore during World War II is etched into the bricks of a popular staircase.

name: LASALLE Campus Green
description: A quiet patch of astroturf laid out between a building that seems to be splitting in half.

name: Old Hill Street Police Station
description: Rainbow shutters decorate this former police station.

name: Keramat Bukit Kasita
description: This royal shrine and cemetery is hidden amidst modern public housing.

name: Changi Jurassic Mile
description: A dinosaur-laden walking trail outside Singapore's Changi Airport.

name: 'Place of Refuge'
description: According to fictional Kcymaerxthaereal history, a powerful woman with a voice whose beauty could not be concealed found solace at this site at the Majestic Hotel in Singapore.

name: People's Park Complex
description: A manifestation of modernist architectural ideas, this massive, multi-purpose building was one of the first of its kind in Southeast Asia.

name: 'Inscription of the Island'
description: An artist crafted this lonely left hand as an imagined relic from Singapore's history.

name: Jalan Kubor Cemetery
description: The oldest Muslim cemetery in Singapore, with a strong royal connection.

name: Jurong Hill
description: This tranquil park with a spiral viewing tower is an off-the-beaten-path area where renowned people have planted a small grove of trees.

name: The Singapore Stone
description: An undeciphered text is inscribed on a mysterious stone slab.

name: Chulalongkorn's Elephant Statue
description: This statue was a gift to commemorate the Thai monarch's first visit to a foreign land.

name: Keramat Iskandar Shah
description: A venerated grave atop an ancient royal hill, often attributed to the last king of Singapura.

name: Tanglin Hill Brunei Hostel
description: A abandoned hostel which was once home to Brunei's brightest students and present-day politicians.

name: Marsiling Tunnels
description: These secretive tunnels were once the site of a Royal Air Force fuel reserve depot.

name: Mother and Child
description: A public statue crafted by Singapore's pioneering sculptor, Ng Eng Teng.

name: Kim Hock Guan
description: The oldest bakkwa shop in Singapore is especially beloved during the Lunar New Year festivities.

name: Long Bar
description: Historic bar where the Singapore Sling cocktail was invented.

name: Nirvana Memorial Garden
description: Surrounded by a serene and tranquil environment, this magnificent columbarium is the popular final resting place for many families to house their departed loved ones.

name: Tortoises of Kusu Island
description: Legend holds that a tortoise turned itself into this island to save two shipwrecked sailors.

name: The Intan
description: A private home museum with a collection of over 5,000 Peranakan artifacts.

name: The Majestic Theatre
description: The elaborate façade of this old Cantonese opera house celebrates the building's history and the love story that founded it.

name: Kubur Kassim Cemetery
description: Old Muslim cemetery and resting place of folkloric creatures

name: ‘From Chettiars to Financiers’
description: A unique, time-transcending work of art depicting the progress of trade in Singapore.

name: 'The River Merchants'
description: These bronze statues depict the early days of trading in Singapore.

name: Sijori Wondergolf
description: An abandoned miniature golf course in the middle of the jungle.

name: Long Ya Men
description: A replica of the lost rock cliff that was once significant in Singapore's maritime history.

name: Young Samsui Woman Mural
description: A mural honoring the women who built Singapore turned a quiet alley into a citywide debate.

name: Fu Lu Shou Complex
description: A one-of-a-kind shopping mall dedicated to Buddhist and Daoist spirituality.

name: Devil's Bend (Old Upper Thomson Road)
description: This sharp turn was once the most dangerous part of the Singapore Grand Prix circuit.

name: Civilian War Memorial
description: A monument to the civilian victims of the Japanese Occupation forces of World War II.

name: Sembawang Hot Springs
description: A hidden hot spring in Singapore with a surprising history.

name: Tuas Lamp Post 1
description: How did a sticker-covered lamp post become a beloved landmark for cyclists in Singapore?

name: Samy's Curry Restaurant
description: One of Singapore's oldest Indian restaurants, specialising in Southern Indian cuisine.

name: Forts of Sentosa
description: Forgotten forts that were pivotal in Singapore’s military history.

name: The Horse Shoe Block
description: One of Singapore’s few remaining building with a WWII air raid shelter.

name: Street of the Dead
description: A street with an infamous past where the terminally ill were sent to die.

name: Umbrella Trees
description: Colorful umbrella-covered trees in Singapore's Little India look straight out of Dr. Seuss.

name: Keppel Hill Reservoir
description: A former reservoir that was only recently discovered after more than 60 years of disuse.

name: The Clinic
description: "Would you like your sangria in an IV bag, or straight-up?"

name: Changi Junk Store
description: A so-called Singapore junk store that is closer to a historical restoration center.

name: BooksActually
description: An independent bookstore and press with a large collection of local and English-language literature.

name: Sungei Road Thieves Market
description: A licentious look into Singapore's past, full of disorderly bric-a-brac and disreputable characters.

name: Singapore Philatelic Museum
description: Postal archives over 150 years old offer a window into Singapore's culture and tradition.

name: Keramat Kusu
description: Three Malay graves are at the center of an annual pilgrimage that reflects syncretic practices.
